rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Courts Order And For Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Apr Real Estate Services, Inc., Compass California Ii, Inc., And Paula Y. Rose
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 21, 2022, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Discovery Sanctions is OFF CALENDAR for Department 501, re-noticed for department 302. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT I BASSIN VS. BRENDAN LOFTUS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment And Seal Record (Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion; Declarration; And (Proposed) Order)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 21, 2022, line 2. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Judgment. Hearing Required. Mr. Loftus and the process server to be present in-person or via Zoom with video capability. The Court is inclined to deny the motion under CCP 473(d) and to grant the motion to restrict access per CCP 1161.2.5. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA DAVIS VS. 999 SUMMIT OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 21, 2022, line 3. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is continued to January 9, 2023. The Court is inclined to grant the motion for leave to add new causes of action if the trial is continued. Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 3 pages) addressing whether CitiScape may be added as a new defendant (not a DOE defendant), i.e. to address the Statue of Limitations no later than December 28, 2022. Defendant may file a response to the supplemental brief (not to exceed 3 pages) no later than January 3, 2023. Briefs shall be limited to the issues expressly authorized by this ruling. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN MCGLYNN ET AL VS. 1000 SUTTER LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-21-594420 WITH CASE CGC-21-596064
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 21, 2022, line 4. Defendants' Motion to Consolidate 594420 and 596064 is DENIED. While there are some common issues of fact and law involved, the parties are different, and the claims are not all the same. Complete consolidation for all purposes is not appropriate. If the parties wish to make a motion to consolidate for limited purposes (e.g. discovery only, trial only, etc.), such motion may be brought at a later time. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGMENT SERVICES VS. JOSEPH ITIEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 21, 2022, line 7. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Notice is in compliance with the law and is not uncertain. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that the law requires any additional information to be included in the Notice or the Complaint to state a cause of action or that there is an absolute bar on attempts to recover rents due after the effective date of SFRO 37.9(a)(1)(E). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
